text.title: Daily Marketing Standup Checklist Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of daily-marketing-standup-checklist enforces. Each rule has a statement, a rationale anchored to industry practice, and a source citation.

rule.statement: Checklist runs in ≤15 minutes; if it takes longer, prune items.
rule.rationale: Beyond 15 minutes the team skips it; tight time-box is the discipline.

rule.statement: Output is a dated, owner-signed Markdown artefact stored in `marketing/standups/YYYY-MM-DD.md`.
rule.rationale: Versioned artefacts make trend analysis possible; ephemeral Slack messages do not.

rule.statement: Top of artefact names the day's one-thing focus in ≤15 words.
rule.rationale: Without a focus, the rest of the day fragments across tools.

rule.statement: Anomaly threshold is explicit: ±30% vs 7-day rolling average flags an investigation.
rule.rationale: Vague anomaly detection produces alert fatigue; ±30% is the smallest move worth noting at this cadence.

rule.statement: Each anomaly gets a one-line hypothesis + an owner; no orphan anomalies.
rule.rationale: Anomaly without owner = anomaly that returns tomorrow.

rule.statement: Stub rule for conclusion 'skip-methodology' referenced from 06-decision-tree.xml. Replace with the real testable rule that resolves this branch of the tree.
rule.rationale: Auto-added by fix-methodology-phase-d.py to keep tree refs resolvable.
